title: Changing order on the x axis
excerpt: Is there a way to choose the order things are appearing on the x-axis.  I have all my data labelled by financial period of the year and would lie the x axis to have the periods along the bottom.  However, when i do this …

posts.author: JCross (John Cross)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there a way to choose the order things are appearing on the x-axis. I have all my data labelled by financial period of the year and would lie the x axis to have the periods along the bottom. However, when i do this is automatically sorts them into ascending order.

I am currently using dates across the bottom but this doesnt really work as the months do not match up with our 12 periods so it gives a false reflection of our income.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
What if you use the ‘Sort By’ in the dashboard settings?

image422×874 25.6 KB

posts.author: Matt_Frost (Dr Matt Frost - www.frostgroup.uk)
posts.cookedHtml: You can only choose ascending or descending, but if you need them in a specific order you can give them a prefix. If your financial year starts in April for example you can use 01 April

posts.author: JCross (John Cross)
posts.cookedHtml: All sorted thanks. Good tip for future ones. Thanks!

posts.author: JCross (John Cross)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you. I thought id already tried this and it hadn’t worked. But i must have done it wrong because it worked this time!
